search_queries_used: site:entnet.org dysphonia guideline 2018 follow-up escalation laryngoscopy outcomes
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic reflux, infection, functional, benign-lesion, or malignancy diagnosis
automatic laryngoscopy, imaging, steroid, antibiotic, reflux medicine, voice therapy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Dysphonia has multiple structural, neurological, inflammatory, functional, and malignant causes.
No investigation, treatment, medicine, procedure, therapy, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children or adults with dysphonia; professional voice users, recent surgery or intubation, neck mass, tobacco exposure, neurological disease, and persistent unexplained symptoms need individualized review.
setting_applicability: Primary, ENT, laryngology, or speech and language therapy follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requires UAE ENT, laryngoscopy, voice therapy, oncology, prescribing, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2018 dysphonia fact sheet and exact key action statements were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2018 update remains the current AAO-HNSF dysphonia guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact dysphonia history section supports interval voice change, onset, functional impact, associated features, relevant negatives, and clinician-performed ENT or neck assessment without diagnosing the cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact escalation section supports clinician concern and review of already available specialist information without generating laryngoscopy or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact outcomes section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending voice therapy, medicine, or surgery.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

unresolved_source_gaps: Voice quality and duration, occupation, smoking, intubation, surgery, reflux and neurological context, laryngeal examination, diagnosis, treatment, therapy, referral, and outcome remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
